Act earlier.

Earlier signal from routine CT.

Core innovation

From CT to lesion localization.

ePAI system overview
1

Routine contrast CT

Uses abdominal CT scans already acquired during standard clinical care.

2

Anatomy segmentation

Localizes pancreas and surrounding structures before looking for lesions.

3

Candidate search

Finds small and subtle suspicious regions rather than predicting from the whole image alone.

4

Lesion localization

Returns a visual target that clinicians can inspect and verify on the CT itself.

5

PDAC classification

Assigns the candidate lesion to PDAC, non-PDAC, or normal for downstream triage.

Prediagnostic CT evidence.

Direct detection

Subtle lesions localized on prediagnostic scans.

Indirect cues

Structural changes detected before clear mass presentation.

Inspectable image target for clinical review.

Key results
ResultValueInterpretation
Internal all-size PDAC AUC0.985High internal diagnostic performance.
Small PDAC sensitivity95.3% internal; 91.5% externalSensitive to small lesions.
External validation7,158 patients; 6 centersValidated across external centers.
Prediagnostic detection75 / 159; median 347 daysPrediagnostic cases detected before clinical diagnosis.
